summary: Frontend-only e2e testing of browser-based 2D fighting game (The Forge + Lobby + Arena). 18/18 critical flows PASS: forge UI loads, pixel canvas drawing, color palette, brush sizes 1-4, eraser, clear all, grid toggle, Fire/Water element toggle, save-disabled-without-name, save weapon to localStorage, Enter Arena reveal, lobby with Host/Join/Practice cards, practice mode launches Phaser canvas with stickmen (Hero + SPARBOT) and weapon/health bars, WASD keys, mouse-click attack, Exit -> Lobby, Back to Forge, Host Game flow generates room code. Multiplayer P2P (Trystero/Nostr) join-with-second-peer was NOT exercised (single browser). Match-end overlay + Rematch button were NOT triggered (would require waiting for full HP depletion).

ui_bugs.issue: React warning in console: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop. Check the render method of div. It was passed a child from Lobby.' Both .map() calls in Lobby.jsx already have key props (key={p.id} and key={`wait-${i}`}), so the warning likely stems from peers connecting with duplicate/empty selfId or an Array.from fragment. Cosmetic only, no functional impact.

issues: Canvas2D performance warning (non-blocking): PixelCanvas getImageData should set willReadFrequently:true on getContext('2d') to silence Chromium warnings (only triggered by tests, not user code path).
WebGL 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' warnings from Phaser - benign but indicates Phaser scene may be performing readbacks (e.g. for snapshots). Not user-visible.

action_items: [LOW] Investigate Lobby key warning - likely caused by a peer entry without a stable id. Consider falling back to crypto.randomUUID() if net.selfId is undefined when rendering the local player card.
[OPTIONAL] Did not exercise full match completion (HP -> 0 -> overlay -> Rematch). Main agent could self-test by lowering AI bot HP or letting test agent run a longer match in next iteration.
[OPTIONAL] Real P2P multiplayer (two browser contexts joining same Nostr room) was not validated end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: TheArena.jsx uses ArenaScene.pendingData as a static side-channel to pass props into Phaser scene - works but is fragile across multiple mounts; consider scene.init(data) via scene.start('ArenaScene', data) instead.
Lobby.jsx: networkRef cleanup runs on unmount but createGameRoom may already be in-flight; ensure leave() is idempotent in lib/networking.js.
PixelCanvas.jsx: getContext('2d') should pass {willReadFrequently:true} since toDataURL/save flow reads pixels.

App.js: when navigating ?room=ABC and no weapon saved, user lands on Forge with pendingRoom note but there's no auto-redirect to Lobby after first save - user must click Enter Arena. Acceptable UX but worth confirming.
test_credentials: None required - fully client-side P2P game
seed_data_creation: Saved a weapon to localStorage during the test ({name:'BladeOfDoom', element:'fire'}) and player name 'Hero'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Forge -> Save weapon -> Enter Arena -> Practice flow is fully validated and the Phaser scene renders Hero (green stickman) vs SPARBOT (cyan stickman with weapon) with HP bars and ground. Untested: (1) actual match-end overlay & rematch (would need to wait for full match), (2) two-browser multiplayer Nostr connection. Use localStorage.setItem('forge_weapon', JSON.stringify({name:'X',element:'fire',texture:'data:image/png;base64,...'})) to skip Forge in future runs.
rca of the issue: No critical issues found. The single console warning about React keys is cosmetic and did not produce any visible misbehavior; all DOM lists in Lobby.jsx already declare keys. Most likely cause: a transient render pass where `players` had an entry whose id was undefined (e.g. before net.selfId resolved). Mitigation: default key to a crypto.randomUUID() fallback when p.id is falsy.
